Galarhoeus jolkinii (similar to Galarhoeus officinalis) is a type of plant in the Cucurbitaceae family, commonly known as a type of ginseng or an adaptogenic plant.

The health benefits of this plant include its ability to act as an adaptogen, helping to reduce stress and improve overall well-being. It is also believed to have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and immunomodulatory effects, which may help to protect against diseases such as cancer, cardiovascular disease, and neurodegenerative disorders.

The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.

The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, tinctures, and capsules, which are often used to promote relaxation, improve sleep quality, and enhance physical performance.
